subject: pakistan-studies
description: Key Muslim Hindu Personalities MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: When was Sir Syed Ahmad Khan born?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed Ahmad Khan was born on October 17, 1817 in Delhi.

mcqs.q: When did Sir Syed Ahmad Khan die?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed Ahmad Khan passed away on March 27, 1898 at Aligarh.

mcqs.q: At the time of 1857 War of Independence, Sir Syed Ahmad Khan was Sadar Amin of?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed was serving as Sadar Amin (Sub-Judge) in Bijnor during the 1857 uprising.

mcqs.q: Sir Syed advised Muslims to stay away from Congress because:
options: Muslims needed time to prepare for political struggle
Its demands were against Muslim interests
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed urged Muslims to focus on education and avoid Congress, which he saw as Hindu-dominated and against Muslim interests.

mcqs.q: Aligarh Movement was started by?
mcqs.explanation: The Aligarh Movement for modern education among Muslims was founded by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan.

mcqs.q: Who established the Scientific Society in 1864?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed founded the Scientific Society in 1864 to translate English scientific works into Urdu.

mcqs.q: When was the Risala Tehzeeb-ul-Ikhlaq published?
mcqs.explanation: The reformist journal Tehzeeb-ul-Ikhlaq was first published by Sir Syed in 1870.

mcqs.q: Who was appointed first Secretary of the Board of Trustees of Aligarh College?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed himself served as the first Secretary of the MAO College Board of Trustees.

mcqs.q: MAO College was founded by?
mcqs.explanation: The Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental (MAO) College at Aligarh was founded in 1877 by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan.

mcqs.q: After independence, MAO College was shifted to which institution in Pakistan?
mcqs.explanation: The MAO heritage continued at Islamia College Lahore, established at the former Sanatan Dharma College Lahore premises.

mcqs.q: MAO College was established in 1877 to:
options: Give Muslims access to modern knowledge
mcqs.explanation: The primary goal of MAO College was to blend Western education with Islamic teachings for Muslim advancement.

mcqs.q: In 1875 the Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental School was founded by:
mcqs.explanation: The MAO School (precursor to the college) was founded in 1875 at Aligarh by Sir Syed.

mcqs.q: Muhammadan Educational Conference was established by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan in:
mcqs.explanation: The All India Muhammadan Educational Conference was established in 1886.

mcqs.q: سرسید نے دو قومی نظریے کی بنیاد کیوں رکھی تھی ؟
options: انگریزوں کے مسلمانوں کے خلاف کاروائیوں سے
الگ وطن کے حصول کے لئے
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed recognised the Hindu-Muslim divide, particularly after the Urdu-Hindi controversy (1867) and biased Hindu attitudes, laying the foundation for the Two-Nation Theory.

mcqs.q: When did Sir Syed Ahmad Khan establish the Indian Patriotic Association?
mcqs.explanation: The Indian Patriotic Association was established by Sir Syed in 1888 to counter Congress propaganda.

mcqs.q: Waqar-ul-Mulk was the ___ of the Aligarh Institute:
mcqs.explanation: Nawab Waqar-ul-Mulk served as Secretary of the MAO College Board of Trustees after Sir Syed's death.

mcqs.q: Nawab Waqar-ul-Mulk became Secretary of the Board of Trustees Aligarh in the year:
mcqs.explanation: Nawab Waqar-ul-Mulk was appointed Secretary in 1907.

mcqs.q: 'Loyal Muhammadans of India' was written by?
mcqs.explanation: 'The Loyal Muhammadans of India' was written by Sir Syed to counter negative British perceptions of Muslims after 1857.

mcqs.q: Who wrote Asar-us-Sanadid?
mcqs.explanation: Asar-us-Sanadid (Monuments of the Great) was written by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan.

mcqs.q: Asar-us-Sanadid is a book about?
mcqs.explanation: The book describes the ancient buildings and topography of Delhi (architectural heritage).

mcqs.q: The book 'Khutbat-e-Ahmadia' is written by:
mcqs.explanation: Khutbat-e-Ahmadiya was written by Sir Syed in response to William Muir's biography of the Prophet (PBUH).

mcqs.q: A pamphlet written by Sir Syed to describe the similarities between Muslims and Christians:
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed wrote 'Tabyin-ul-Kalam' to establish common ground between Islam and Christianity.

mcqs.q: Sir Syed Ahmad Khan is buried in:
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed is buried near the mosque on the Aligarh Muslim University campus.

mcqs.q: 'Hayat-e-Javed' by Altaf Hussain Hali is on the life of:
mcqs.explanation: 'Hayat-e-Javed' is the famous biography of Sir Syed Ahmad Khan written by Altaf Hussain Hali.

mcqs.q: Mirza Ghalib was a contemporary of:
mcqs.explanation: Mirza Ghalib and Sir Syed Ahmad Khan lived in Delhi at the same time.

mcqs.q: What was Sir Syed Ahmad Khan's real name?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed's birth name was simply Syed Ahmad; 'Khan' was a family suffix added later.

mcqs.q: What was the name of Sir Syed Ahmad Khan's mother?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed Ahmad Khan's mother was Aziz-un-Nisa Begum.

mcqs.q: Where did Sir Syed Ahmad Khan's father hold a special position?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed's father held a position in the court of Mughal Emperor Akbar Shah II.

mcqs.q: In which year did Sir Syed Ahmad Khan join the East India Company?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed joined the judicial service of the East India Company in 1839.

mcqs.q: When was Sir Syed Ahmad Khan promoted to the position of Munshi?
mcqs.explanation: He was promoted to the position of Munshi in 1841.

mcqs.q: Where was Sir Syed Ahmad Khan transferred as Deputy Judge in 1846?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed was transferred to Delhi as Deputy Judge in 1846.

mcqs.q: During which year was Sir Syed appointed Assistant Collector in Bijnor?
mcqs.explanation: He was appointed Assistant Collector in Bijnor in 1855.

mcqs.q: During which historical event was Sir Syed Ahmad Khan in Bijnor?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed was posted at Bijnor during the War of Independence (1857).

mcqs.q: Where was Sir Syed transferred after the 1857 War of Independence?
mcqs.explanation: After 1857 Sir Syed was transferred to Muradabad.

mcqs.q: When did Sir Syed Ahmad Khan establish Gulshan School in Muradabad?
mcqs.explanation: Gulshan School in Muradabad was founded in 1859.

mcqs.q: In which year was Victoria School founded in Ghazipur by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan?
mcqs.explanation: Victoria School in Ghazipur was founded in 1863.

mcqs.q: What was the purpose of the Scientific Society founded in 1864?
options: Translating English books into local languages
mcqs.explanation: The Society's main goal was to translate scientific English works into Urdu.

mcqs.q: Who inaugurated MAO College in 1877?
mcqs.explanation: The foundation stone of MAO College was laid by Viceroy Lord Lytton.

mcqs.q: Who was the first principal of MAO College?
mcqs.explanation: The first principal of MAO College was Theodore Beck.

mcqs.q: In which year did Sir Syed Ahmad Khan retire as Chief Judge?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed retired from judicial service in 1878.

mcqs.q: When did Sir Syed Ahmad Khan become a member of the Imperial Legislative Council?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed was nominated to the Imperial Legislative Council in 1878.

mcqs.q: In which year did Sir Syed visit London with his son?
mcqs.explanation: He visited London for 17 months starting in 1869.

mcqs.q: What honour was conferred upon Sir Syed Ahmad Khan in London?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed was conferred Knight Commander of the Star of India (KCSI), i.e. Knighthood.

mcqs.q: Which book did Sir Syed Ahmad Khan write in 1840?
mcqs.explanation: Jam-e-Jam (1840) was among Sir Syed's earliest works.

mcqs.q: What period does the book 'Jam-e-Jam' cover?
mcqs.explanation: Jam-e-Jam covers the Mughal period from Taimur to the last Mughal king Akbar II.

mcqs.q: Which book by Sir Syed is a response to William Muir's objections?
mcqs.explanation: Khutbat-e-Ahmadiya was Sir Syed's response to Muir's controversial biography of the Prophet (PBUH).

mcqs.q: In which year was 'Risala Asbab-e-Baghawat-e-Hind' written?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Syed's pamphlet detailing causes of the 1857 Revolt was written in 1859.

mcqs.q: The main focus of 'Loyal Muhammadans of India' is:
options: Explaining Muslims' loyalty to the British
mcqs.explanation: The book aimed to prove that Muslims were loyal subjects of the British Crown after 1857.

mcqs.q: When was the first edition of 'Tehzeeb-ul-Akhlaq' published?
mcqs.explanation: Tehzeeb-ul-Akhlaq was first published in 1870.

mcqs.q: In which year was 'Asar-us-Sanadid' first published?
mcqs.explanation: Asar-us-Sanadid was published in 1847.

mcqs.q: What is described in the book 'Asar-us-Sanadid'?
mcqs.explanation: The book is a detailed account of the ancient monuments and ruins of Delhi.

mcqs.q: When was the foundation of the political wing of MAO College laid?
mcqs.explanation: The Indian Patriotic Association (political precursor) was established in 1886.

mcqs.q: Where was the political wing of MAO College established?
mcqs.explanation: Political efforts centred around Aligarh, the site of MAO College.

mcqs.q: Who became Secretary of the Aligarh organization after Sir Syed's death?
mcqs.explanation: Nawab Waqar-ul-Mulk succeeded Mohsin-ul-Mulk as Secretary of the MAO College Board of Trustees.

mcqs.q: Quaid-e-Azam was born in:
mcqs.explanation: Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah was born on December 25, 1876 in Karachi.

mcqs.q: Wazir Mansion is the birthplace of which person?
mcqs.explanation: Wazir Mansion in Karachi is the birthplace of Muhammad Ali Jinnah.

mcqs.q: Residency of Quaid-e-Azam in Quetta, where he stayed in his last days, was built in:
mcqs.explanation: Ziarat Residency (Quetta) was built in 1892.

mcqs.q: Quaid-e-Azam enrolled in which school?
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah enrolled at Sindh Madrasa-tul-Islam in Karachi.

mcqs.q: When was Quaid-e-Azam first appointed as Third Presidency Magistrate in Bombay?
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah was appointed Third Presidency Magistrate in Bombay in 1900.

mcqs.q: In which year did Quaid-e-Azam join the Indian National Congress?
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah joined the Indian National Congress in 1906.

mcqs.q: When did Quaid-e-Azam join the Muslim League?
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah formally joined the All India Muslim League on 10 October 1913.

mcqs.q: Who was, at one time, called the 'Ambassador of Hindu-Muslim Unity'?
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah was famously given this title during his early political career.

mcqs.q: The title of 'Ambassador of Hindu-Muslim Unity' was given to Quaid-e-Azam by:
mcqs.explanation: The title was conferred by the poet and political activist Sarojini Naidu.

mcqs.q: Quaid-e-Azam resigned from the Imperial Legislative Council due to:
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah resigned from the Imperial Legislative Council in 1919 in protest against the Rowlatt Act.

mcqs.q: Quaid-e-Azam left the Congress in:
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah left Congress in 1920 over differences with Gandhi's Non-Cooperation Movement.

mcqs.q: Quaid-e-Azam left the Indian National Congress due to which movement?
mcqs.explanation: He disagreed with Gandhi's Non-Cooperation Movement tactics.

mcqs.q: What was the duration of overlapping membership of Quaid-e-Azam in both Muslim League and Congress?
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah was in Congress (1906-1920) and the League (1913-1947), giving an overlap of about 7 years (1913-1920).

mcqs.q: Quaid-e-Azam's 14 Points were a response to:
mcqs.explanation: The Fourteen Points (1929) were formulated as a counter-proposal to the 1928 Nehru Report.

mcqs.q: What is the name of Quaid-e-Azam's daughter?
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah's only daughter was Dina Jinnah (Wadia).

mcqs.q: Quaid-e-Azam addressed the Constituent Assembly for the first time on:
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah delivered his famous address on 11 August 1947 on equality and religious freedom.

mcqs.q: Quaid-e-Azam inaugurated on 1st July 1948:
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah inaugurated the State Bank of Pakistan on 1 July 1948.

mcqs.q: Qazi Muhammad Isa was a close friend of:
mcqs.explanation: Qazi Muhammad Isa was a close associate of Jinnah, especially in Balochistan politics.

mcqs.q: Who said: 'The Instrument of Accession to India is illegal and fraudulent'?
mcqs.explanation: The statement on the Kashmir Instrument of Accession is attributed to Jinnah.

mcqs.q: Why did Quaid-e-Azam say Kashmir is a lifeline of Pakistan?
options: It is the most beautiful place
All five major rivers of Pakistan originate from Kashmir
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah called Kashmir Pakistan's 'lifeline' because its rivers flow into Pakistan.

mcqs.q: At the time of his death, what was the age of Quaid-e-Azam?
mcqs.explanation: Quaid-e-Azam was 71 years old at his death in September 1948 (born December 1876).

mcqs.q: Where is Quaid-e-Azam's tomb situated?
mcqs.explanation: Quaid-e-Azam's mausoleum (Mazar-e-Quaid) is located in Karachi.

mcqs.q: The foundation stone of Quaid-e-Azam's Mausoleum was laid by Field Marshal Ayub Khan in:
mcqs.explanation: Field Marshal Ayub Khan laid the foundation stone in July 1960.

mcqs.q: Who designed the mausoleum of Quaid-e-Azam?
mcqs.explanation: The design was developed by Indian architect Yahya Merchant.

mcqs.q: Stanley Wolpert is famous for his biography of:
mcqs.explanation: Stanley Wolpert is famous for his critical biography, 'Jinnah of Pakistan'.

mcqs.q: Who wrote the official biography of Quaid-e-Azam?
mcqs.explanation: Hector Bolitho authored the official biography 'Jinnah: Creator of Pakistan'.

mcqs.q: Which tree did Quaid-e-Azam like the most?
mcqs.explanation: The Sanober (Pine) tree is associated with Jinnah's preference at Ziarat Residency.

mcqs.q: The Area Study Centre at Quaid-e-Azam University is about:
mcqs.explanation: The Area Study Centre at Quaid-e-Azam University focuses on Latin America.

mcqs.q: Quaid-e-Azam's Mausoleum is an impressive landmark of:
mcqs.explanation: Mazar-e-Quaid is located in Karachi.

mcqs.q: Quaid-e-Azam's personal letters are preserved at:
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah's private papers are housed in the National Archives of Pakistan, Islamabad.

mcqs.q: Who is known as 'The Father of the Nation'?
mcqs.explanation: Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah is the Father of the Nation of Pakistan.

mcqs.q: Mother language of Quaid-e-Azam was?
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah's mother tongue was Gujarati.

mcqs.q: Who was Muhammad Ali Jinnah's first wife?
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah's first wife, married in his youth, was Emibai.

mcqs.q: When did Jinnah go to London for legal studies?
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah left for London in 1892 to study law.

mcqs.q: Which institution did Jinnah join in London to study law?
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah enrolled at Lincoln's Inn, London.

mcqs.q: When did Muhammad Ali Jinnah resign from the Imperial Legislative Council?
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah resigned in 1919 in protest against the Rowlatt Act.

mcqs.q: In which year did Jinnah return permanently from London and become Muslim League's permanent president?
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah returned permanently to India from London in 1934 to lead the Muslim League.

mcqs.q: What significant event occurred on 16th August 1946?
mcqs.explanation: The Muslim League observed Direct Action Day on 16 August 1946.

mcqs.q: How many times did Quaid-e-Azam visit Sarhad (NWFP) province?
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah visited NWFP (Sarhad) three times to mobilise support for Pakistan.

mcqs.q: Who first gave Muhammad Ali Jinnah the title of 'Quaid-e-Azam'?
mcqs.explanation: The title is initially attributed to Mazhar-ul-Haq.

mcqs.q: In which year was Muhammad Ali Jinnah given the title of 'Quaid-e-Azam'?
mcqs.explanation: The title gained widespread currency after the Sindh Muslim League resolution in 1938.

mcqs.q: Who raised the slogan 'Quaid-e-Azam Zindabad' for the first time?
mcqs.explanation: The slogan was coined by Mian Ferozuddin Ahmad.

mcqs.q: In which year was 'Quaid-e-Azam Zindabad' first raised?
mcqs.explanation: The slogan became popular during the independence struggle in 1947.

mcqs.q: What illness did Muhammad Ali Jinnah suffer from in his final days?
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah suffered from Tuberculosis (T.B.).

mcqs.q: Where was Jinnah relocated during his illness for better health?
mcqs.explanation: He was relocated to Ziarat Residency in Balochistan.

mcqs.q: Who authored 'Last Days of Quaid-e-Azam'?
mcqs.explanation: The book was written by Jinnah's personal physician, Colonel Ilahi Bakhsh.

mcqs.q: 'Jinnah: The Sole Spokesman' was written by:
mcqs.explanation: The seminal work on Jinnah was written by Dr. Ayesha Jalal.

mcqs.q: Who wrote the book 'Jinnah of Pakistan'?
mcqs.explanation: Stanley Wolpert authored 'Jinnah of Pakistan'.

mcqs.q: The book 'Jinnah: Creator of Pakistan' was written by?
mcqs.explanation: Hector Bolitho wrote 'Jinnah: Creator of Pakistan'.

mcqs.q: Who directed the movie 'Jinnah'?
mcqs.explanation: The film 'Jinnah' was directed by Jamil Dehlavi.

mcqs.q: Which actor played the role of Quaid-e-Azam in the movie 'Jinnah'?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Christopher Lee played Jinnah in the 1998 film.

mcqs.q: Who administered the oath to Quaid-e-Azam as Pakistan's first Governor-General?
mcqs.explanation: Justice Mian Abdul Rashid administered the oath.

mcqs.q: On 9th November 1877, who was born?
mcqs.explanation: Allama Muhammad Iqbal was born on 9 November 1877 in Sialkot.

mcqs.q: What was the name of Allama Iqbal's mother?
mcqs.explanation: Allama Iqbal's mother was Imam Bibi.

mcqs.q: When did Allama Iqbal go to England for higher studies?
mcqs.explanation: Iqbal travelled to England for higher education in 1905.

mcqs.q: In 1923, Allama Iqbal published a book titled:
mcqs.explanation: Payam-e-Mashriq was published in 1923.

mcqs.q: In December 1930, Allama Iqbal delivered his famous address in which city?
mcqs.explanation: Iqbal delivered his Presidential Address at the Muslim League's Allahabad session.

mcqs.q: Allama Iqbal delivered his famous Allahabad Address in:
mcqs.explanation: The Allahabad Address was delivered in 1930.

mcqs.q: When did Allama Iqbal become President of the All India Muslim League session?
mcqs.explanation: He presided over the League session in Allahabad in 1930.

mcqs.q: Who is the national poet of Pakistan?
mcqs.explanation: Allama Muhammad Iqbal is Pakistan's national poet (Shair-e-Mashriq).

mcqs.q: 'Khudi' was a key concept of:
mcqs.explanation: The concept of 'Khudi' (Selfhood) is central to Allama Iqbal's philosophy.

mcqs.q: Pakistan's national poet Allama Iqbal died on:
mcqs.explanation: Allama Iqbal died on 21 April 1938 in Lahore.

mcqs.q: Who gave the title of 'Shair-e-Mashriq' to Allama Iqbal?
mcqs.explanation: The title is attributed to Maulana Shibli Naumani.

mcqs.q: From which school did Allama Iqbal complete matriculation?
mcqs.explanation: Iqbal matriculated from Scotch Mission High School, Sialkot.

mcqs.q: Where did Allama Iqbal earn his PhD?
mcqs.explanation: Iqbal obtained his PhD from Ludwig Maximilian University, Munich, Germany.

mcqs.q: What was the title of Allama Iqbal's PhD thesis?
options: The Development of Metaphysics in Persia
mcqs.explanation: His thesis was 'The Development of Metaphysics in Persia'.

mcqs.q: Who gave Allama Iqbal the title 'Hakim-ul-Ummat'?
mcqs.explanation: The title 'Hakim-ul-Ummat' is associated with Khwaja Hasan Nizami.

mcqs.q: Which teacher of Allama Iqbal was awarded the title 'Shams-ul-Ulama'?
mcqs.explanation: Maulvi Mir Hassan, Iqbal's teacher, was honoured with 'Shams-ul-Ulama'.

mcqs.q: When did Allama Iqbal first join the Muslim League?
mcqs.explanation: Iqbal first joined the Muslim League in 1908.

mcqs.q: From which institution did Allama Iqbal earn a degree in Economics & Political Science?
mcqs.explanation: Iqbal earned this degree from the London School of Economics.

mcqs.q: In which year was Allama Iqbal awarded the title 'Sir'?
mcqs.explanation: He was knighted in 1922.

mcqs.q: What was the title of the poem recited by Allama Iqbal at the Anjuman Himayat-e-Islam session in 1911?
mcqs.explanation: He recited 'Tuloo-e-Islam' (The Rise of Islam) in 1911.

mcqs.q: How many wives did Allama Iqbal have?
mcqs.explanation: Allama Iqbal had three wives in his lifetime.

mcqs.q: Who is Allama Iqbal's son?
mcqs.explanation: Dr. Javed Iqbal was Allama Iqbal's son and a noted jurist.

mcqs.q: Which book was authored by Javed Iqbal (Allama Iqbal's son)?
mcqs.explanation: 'Zinda Rud' (biography of his father) was written by Javed Iqbal.

mcqs.q: Who is Allama Iqbal's grandson and a Pakistani Senator?
mcqs.explanation: Walid Iqbal is Allama Iqbal's grandson and a politician.

mcqs.q: For which ruler did Allama Iqbal write 'Payam-e-Mashriq'?
mcqs.explanation: Payam-e-Mashriq was dedicated to Amir Amanullah Khan of Afghanistan.

mcqs.q: What role did Allama Iqbal play in Afghanistan's education policy?
mcqs.explanation: Iqbal helped draft educational policies for Afghanistan during his 1933 visit.

mcqs.q: Who led the prayer in which Allama Iqbal participated during his Afghanistan visit?
mcqs.explanation: He prayed alongside Afghan King Shah Amanullah Khan.

mcqs.q: Maulana Muhammad Ali Johar was a student of:
mcqs.explanation: Maulana Muhammad Ali Johar was a prominent alumnus of Aligarh Muslim University.

mcqs.q: In 1911 Maulana Muhammad Ali Johar started the newspaper 'Comrade' from:
mcqs.explanation: He started the English newspaper 'Comrade' from Calcutta in 1911.

mcqs.q: In which year did Maulana Muhammad Ali Johar start publishing 'Hamdard'?
mcqs.explanation: He started the Urdu newspaper 'Hamdard' in 1913.

mcqs.q: In which city was 'Hamdard' newspaper first published?
mcqs.explanation: The Urdu newspaper 'Hamdard' was published from Delhi.

mcqs.q: Maulana Muhammad Ali Johar is buried in:
mcqs.explanation: Maulana Muhammad Ali Johar is buried in Jerusalem, Palestine.

mcqs.q: When was Maulana Muhammad Ali Johar born?
mcqs.explanation: He was born in 1878 at Rampur.

mcqs.q: In which year did Maulana Muhammad Ali Johar become President of Congress?
mcqs.explanation: He presided over the Congress session at Kakinada in 1923.

mcqs.q: Who persuaded Quaid-e-Azam to join the All India Muslim League?
mcqs.explanation: Maulana Muhammad Ali Johar (with Syed Wazir Hasan) persuaded Jinnah to join the League.

mcqs.q: Who among these is considered one of the most versatile personalities of the Subcontinent?
mcqs.explanation: Maulana Muhammad Ali Johar was journalist, poet, orator and politician.

mcqs.q: Maulana Muhammad Ali Johar passed away during which event?
mcqs.explanation: He died in London in January 1931 while attending the First Round Table Conference.

mcqs.q: What was the document considered the constitution of the Muslim League in 1907?
mcqs.explanation: The Muslim League's constitution was often called the 'Blue Book'.

mcqs.q: What was the title of Maulana Muhammad Ali Johar?
mcqs.explanation: He was titled 'Raees-ul-Ahrar' (The Chief of the Free).

mcqs.q: Congress was established in:
mcqs.explanation: The Indian National Congress was founded in 1885 at Bombay.

mcqs.q: In which city was the Indian National Congress founded?
mcqs.explanation: The Congress was founded in Bombay (now Mumbai).

mcqs.q: The Indian National Congress was formed on 28 December 1885 by British civil servant:
mcqs.explanation: Allan Octavian Hume, a retired British civil servant, founded the INC.

mcqs.q: A.O. Hume, on whose initiative the Indian National Congress was formed, was:
options: A former member of Civil Service
mcqs.explanation: A.O. Hume was a former member of the Indian Civil Service.

mcqs.q: What was the purpose of forming the All India National Congress?
options: A platform for civic and political dialogue among educated Indians
mcqs.explanation: The primary purpose was a safety-valve forum for educated Indians on civic/political issues.

mcqs.q: First President of Indian National Congress was:
mcqs.explanation: Womesh Chandra Bannerjee (W.C. Bannerjee) was the first INC President in 1885.

mcqs.q: Who was the first Muslim President of the Indian National Congress?
mcqs.explanation: Badruddin Tyabji presided over the 1887 INC session.

mcqs.q: Who was the second President of the Indian National Congress?
mcqs.explanation: Dadabhai Naoroji presided over the 1886 INC session.

mcqs.q: Dadabhai Naoroji worked for India under the Indian National Movement in:
mcqs.explanation: Dadabhai Naoroji was the first Asian elected to the British Parliament, working from the UK.

mcqs.q: Who was the leader of Hindu Mahasabha?
mcqs.explanation: Madan Mohan Malaviya was a key figure of the Hindu Mahasabha.

mcqs.q: Quaid-e-Azam resigned from Congress in the year:
mcqs.explanation: Jinnah resigned from Congress in 1920.

mcqs.q: The Congress declared complete independence for India as its goal at which session?
mcqs.explanation: The 'Purna Swaraj' resolution was passed at the Lahore session in 1929.

mcqs.q: Why did Muslims celebrate Direct Action Day?
mcqs.explanation: Direct Action Day (1946) was observed to press for a separate Muslim homeland against British/Congress designs.

mcqs.q: Indian National Congress celebrated its Golden Jubilee in which city?
mcqs.explanation: The 50th session (Golden Jubilee) was held in Bombay/Mumbai.

mcqs.q: In which year did the Congress demand 'Purna Swaraj'?
mcqs.explanation: The demand for complete self-rule was formalised in 1929.

mcqs.q: Who were the moderate leaders of the Congress?
options: Lala Lajpat Rai, Bal Gangadhar Tilak
mcqs.explanation: Gopal Krishna Gokhale and Dadabhai Naoroji were the prominent moderates.

mcqs.q: Who among these was an extremist leader of the Indian National Congress?
mcqs.explanation: Bal Gangadhar Tilak was one of the key extremist (Assertive Nationalist) leaders.

mcqs.q: What was the Surat Split of 1907?
options: Division between Congress and Muslim League
Split between moderates and extremists in Congress
mcqs.explanation: The Surat Split divided the INC into moderate and extremist factions in 1907.

mcqs.q: Who founded the Hindu Mahasabha in 1915?
mcqs.explanation: The Hindu Mahasabha was founded by leaders including Madan Mohan Malaviya.

mcqs.q: Who started the Indian Home Rule League in Maharashtra?
mcqs.explanation: Bal Gangadhar Tilak started the Home Rule League in Maharashtra; Annie Besant started the Madras branch.

mcqs.q: Who signed the Lucknow Pact (1916) from Congress?
mcqs.explanation: A.C. Mazumdar was INC President at the 1916 Lucknow session and signed the pact.

mcqs.q: When did Gandhi merge the Indian Home Rule League into Congress?
mcqs.explanation: Gandhi merged the Home Rule League with the INC in 1920.

mcqs.q: Who among these was the first Asian to translate the Quran into English?
mcqs.explanation: Allama Abdullah Yusuf Ali produced the most widely read English translation of the Quran by an Asian Muslim.

mcqs.q: 'Syed Mehdi Ali' was the real name of:
mcqs.explanation: Syed Mehdi Ali was the real name of Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk (commonly noted; though some sources confuse with Waqar-ul-Mulk).

mcqs.q: What was the real name of Nawab Waqar-ul-Mulk?
mcqs.explanation: Nawab Waqar-ul-Mulk's real name was Mushtaq Hussain Zuberi.

mcqs.q: Real name of the Raja Sahib of Mahmudabad was:
mcqs.explanation: Raja Amir Ahmad Khan was the Raja of Mahmudabad.

mcqs.q: Who is called the 'Frontier Gandhi'?
mcqs.explanation: Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan was nicknamed 'Frontier Gandhi'.

mcqs.q: Who is called the 'Sarhadi Gandhi'?
mcqs.explanation: Sarhadi Gandhi is another title for Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan.

mcqs.q: Who coined the name of 'Pakistan'?
mcqs.explanation: Chaudhry Rehmat Ali coined the name 'Pakistan' in his 1933 pamphlet 'Now or Never'.

mcqs.q: In which year did Chaudhry Rehmat Ali coin the name 'Pakistan'?
mcqs.explanation: The name was coined in 1933 in his pamphlet 'Now or Never'.

mcqs.q: Who was the last Prime Minister of United Punjab?
mcqs.explanation: Malik Khizar Hayat Tiwana was the last PM of United Punjab.

mcqs.q: The Unionist Party of Punjab was formed by:
mcqs.explanation: The Unionist Party was founded by Sir Fazl-i-Hussain and Sir Chhotu Ram in 1923.

mcqs.q: Madar-e-Millat Fatima Jinnah died in the year:
mcqs.explanation: Fatima Jinnah died on 9 July 1967 in Karachi.

mcqs.q: Who is known as the 'Mother of the Nation' of Pakistan?
mcqs.explanation: Fatima Jinnah is known as Madar-e-Millat (Mother of the Nation).

mcqs.q: The profession of Fatima Jinnah was:
mcqs.explanation: Fatima Jinnah was a professional dentist trained at the University of Calcutta.

mcqs.q: What was Begum Shaista Ikramullah's profession?
mcqs.explanation: She was a prominent politician, diplomat and writer.

mcqs.q: Who was the first Pakistani to win a Nobel Prize?
mcqs.explanation: Dr. Abdus Salam won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1979 — the first Pakistani laureate.

mcqs.q: In which year did Dr. Abdus Salam win the Nobel Prize in Physics?
mcqs.explanation: Dr. Abdus Salam won the Nobel Prize in 1979 for electroweak unification.

mcqs.q: Dr. Abdul Qadeer Khan did his PhD from Leuven University of:
mcqs.explanation: Dr. A.Q. Khan earned his PhD from Catholic University of Leuven, Belgium.
